g.text: Join Order Optimization: DP Planner Picks Smallest Intermediate Results

text.tspan: Green = chosen optimal plan | Red = rejected suboptimal plan
Formula: rows(A join B) = rows(A) x rows(B) / max(NDV_A, NDV_B)
Cost unit = estimated page reads (disk I/O)
NDV = Number of Distinct Values in join column

g.text: Error multiplies at each join boundary: 2x => 4x => 8x for 3 joins
One stale statistic can cascade to wrong plan choice entirely
Fix: run ANALYZE after bulk INSERT or DELETE operations
DP Optimizer: cost(S) = min over all binary splits of S
Enumerate all subsets bottom-up (2^n states for n tables)
cost({B_filt, A, C}) — try all splits:
Winner: join B_filtered+A first — smallest intermediate at each step
Step 1: B_filtered ⋈ A (apply WHERE filter first)
Filtered B drives join — tiny input set
Full tables joined — no predicate pushdown
